mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing equity investors to devalue corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where unemployment rises above the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, boosting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series deviates from its 12-month moving average by less than 1 standard deviation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during periods of supply chain bottlenecks or when GDP growth falls below 1.5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit margins and dividend payouts, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no statistical correlation with GDP growth rates over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid acceleration in consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations to produce negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in more than 70% of months where inflation exceeds the threshold due to inflation-hedging capital flows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and retail sales validates wage-driven consumption cycles, reinforcing earnings growth forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative coefficient in multivariate regression when tested on 2010-2017 historical data.

mechanism_narrative: Surges in housing starts trigger multiplier effects across construction and durable goods sectors, generating immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Housing starts fail to predict short-term equity returns when mortgage rates exceed 5.0% annually.
